california/aetna.intro_html:
If you have Aetna coverage and you're looking for addiction or mental-health treatment in {state}, your insurance may help pay for rehab, therapy, medication, or other services. Aetna offers different plan types—HMO, PPO, and others—and each has its own network, benefits, and out-of-pocket costs, so what's covered depends on your specific plan.

{business} is a free service that helps you understand what your Aetna plan covers and connects you with treatment options across {state}. We don't provide treatment ourselves, but we can help you navigate your benefits so you can find care that works for you.
california/aetna.intro2_html:
{state} has a wide range of addiction and mental-health treatment settings—from inpatient rehab and detox to outpatient programs and counseling—and many participate in Aetna networks. To access your benefits, you'll typically need to verify coverage with Aetna first, confirm whether a facility or provider is in-network, and check what you'll owe out of pocket.

The process can feel overwhelming, especially if you're in crisis or feeling stuck. That's where we come in. Call or text us, and we'll help you decode your Aetna benefits, answer questions about treatment options near you, and connect you with programs that accept your insurance.

feature_points.heading: Verify your Aetna benefits first
feature_points.text: Call Aetna's customer service or check your member portal to confirm what addiction and mental-health treatment your plan covers—including deductibles, copays, and any prior authorization requirements.

feature_points.heading: Check in-network vs. out-of-network
feature_points.text: Aetna's network varies by plan and region within {state}, so ask whether a specific facility or provider is in-network before you commit, since out-of-network care may cost significantly more.

feature_points.heading: We help you connect the dots
feature_points.text: Once you know what your Aetna plan covers, we can help you find treatment options in {state} that accept your insurance and suit your needs.

faq.q: Does my Aetna plan in {state} cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Most Aetna plans include some coverage for addiction and mental-health treatment, but benefits vary widely depending on your plan type, employer, or marketplace selection. You'll need to check your specific plan documents or call Aetna directly to confirm what's covered—including inpatient, outpatient, medication, and therapy services.

faq.q: How do I find in-network rehab providers that accept Aetna in {state}?
faq.a: Start by calling Aetna's customer service or logging into your member portal to search their {state} provider directory for addiction treatment specialists and facilities. You can also call us—we'll help you verify in-network options and answer questions about what your plan covers for different treatment settings.

faq.q: What will my out-of-pocket costs be with Aetna?
faq.a: Out-of-pocket costs depend on your specific plan, deductible, copays, coinsurance, and whether you use in-network or out-of-network providers. We can't guarantee amounts, but Aetna customer service can give you an estimate once you've found a specific treatment program or provider.

faq.q: Do I need prior authorization from Aetna before starting treatment in {state}?
faq.a: Many Aetna plans require prior authorization for inpatient or intensive outpatient treatment, though rules vary by plan. Check your plan documents or ask Aetna directly—and let the treatment program know you have Aetna, because they often handle the authorization request on your behalf.

california/aetna.local_note: {state} has many treatment providers and facilities that accept Aetna insurance across urban, suburban, and rural areas. Call us to verify your benefits and find options near you.

Using your Medicaid benefits means you can get treatment without worrying about impossible bills. The key is confirming your coverage details upfront so you know what to expect when you call a program—and we're here to help you do that.

feature_points.heading: Verify your Medicaid benefits first
feature_points.text: Your coverage amount, deductible, and copay (if any) depend on your specific Medi-Cal plan and county, so it's worth a quick call to your insurance card or plan before reaching out to treatment programs.

feature_points.heading: Many {state} programs accept Medicaid
feature_points.text: From the Bay Area to San Diego and everywhere in between, treatment facilities across the state work with Medicaid—you just need to find ones that fit your needs and location.

feature_points.heading: {business} connects you, not treats you
feature_points.text: We're a free informational and referral service; we help you understand your benefits and find programs that accept Medicaid, but we don't provide treatment ourselves.

faq.q: Does Medicaid cover drug and alcohol rehab in {state}?
faq.a: Yes. Medi-Cal ({state}'s Medicaid program) covers a range of addiction treatment services, including detoxification, residential rehab, outpatient programs, and medication-assisted treatment. What you're eligible for and what you'll pay depend on your specific plan and county.

faq.q: How do I check what my Medicaid plan covers in {state}?
faq.a: Call the customer service number on your Medicaid card or log into your plan's online portal to ask about addiction treatment benefits, copays, and deductibles. You can also tell us a bit about your situation, and {business} can help you think through the questions to ask.

faq.q: Will I have to pay anything out of pocket with Medicaid?
faq.a: It depends on your specific Medi-Cal plan. Some plans cover treatment with no cost-sharing; others may have a small copay or require you to meet a deductible first. Always verify your benefits directly with your plan before starting treatment.

faq.q: How do I find treatment programs in {state} that accept Medicaid?
faq.a: {business} helps you identify programs and verify that they accept your Medicaid plan. We then connect you with treatment options so you can reach out directly. Note that we're a referral service, not a provider or insurance company, and admission is never guaranteed.

california/medicaid.local_note: Medicaid-accepting treatment programs are available across {state}, including residential, outpatient, and telehealth options. {business} can help you find programs near you and verify that they accept your Medi-Cal plan.

faq.q: Does Medicare cover addiction treatment in {state}?
faq.a: Yes, Medicare Part A and Part B cover medically necessary addiction and mental-health treatment at participating providers and facilities. Coverage details vary by plan and service type, so you'll want to verify your specific benefits before scheduling.

faq.a: Have your Medicare card available and contact {business}—we can help you understand your coverage and find treatment options that accept Medicare in your area. If you're in crisis, call or text 988 or call 911.
